_comment.purpose: Least-privilege IAM role for EC2 instances managed by AWS Systems Manager.
design_notes: This role grants only the permissions required for SSM core functions: agent registration, Run Command, Session Manager, and Patch Manager.
No wildcard actions (*) are used except where AWS requires it for SSM message channel operations.
S3 and CloudWatch permissions are scoped to specific resource prefixes to limit blast radius.
This role does NOT include broad EC2, S3, or admin permissions.
For instances that need additional access (e.g. application-specific S3 buckets), attach a separate role policy rather than expanding this baseline.
compliance_notes: Follows AWS least-privilege IAM design principles.
Satisfies NIST SP 800-53 AC-6 (Least Privilege) and AC-3 (Access Enforcement).
All permissions are documented with operational justification below.

trust_policy._comment: Allows EC2 instances to assume this role via the instance metadata service.

Statement._comment: Required for SSM Agent to register the instance with Systems Manager and receive configuration.
Statement._resource_note: SSM agent operations require * resource scope — AWS does not support resource-level restrictions on these actions.

Statement._comment: Required for Session Manager to establish encrypted shell sessions. Replaces SSH/bastion host access.
Statement._resource_note: ssmmessages actions do not support resource-level restrictions per AWS documentation.

Statement._comment: Required for Run Command to deliver commands to the instance and receive responses.
Statement._resource_note: ec2messages actions do not support resource-level restrictions per AWS documentation.

Statement._comment: Required for Patch Manager to scan instance patch state and report compliance.
Allows SSM to write session logs and Run Command output to CloudWatch Logs for audit retention.
Statement._resource_note: Scoped to /ssm/* log group prefix. Expand prefix only if app teams require a different log group path.

Statement._comment: Allows the log-collector SSM document to upload compressed log archives to the designated bucket. Scoped to ssm-log-collections prefix only.
Statement._resource_note: Replace YOUR-LOG-BUCKET-NAME with your actual bucket name. Do not use * for the bucket name.

Statement._comment: Required if Session Manager is configured to encrypt session data stored in S3.
Required if Session Manager uses a customer-managed KMS key for session encryption. Remove this block if using AWS-managed keys.
Statement._resource_note: Replace with your KMS key ARN. Scoped to specific key — never use * for KMS resource.

step_1_create_role._comment: Create the IAM role with the trust policy above.

step_2_attach_managed_policy._comment: The AWS managed policy covers all core SSM agent permissions and is kept current by AWS.

step_3_attach_custom_policy._comment: Attach the custom inline policy for scoped S3, CloudWatch, and KMS permissions.
step_3_attach_custom_policy.command: aws iam put-role-policy --role-name SSMInstanceRole --policy-name SSMCustomPermissions --policy-document file://iam/ssm-instance-profile.json

step_4_create_instance_profile._comment: EC2 instances require an instance profile wrapper around the IAM role.

step_5_attach_to_instance._comment: Attach the instance profile to a running or new EC2 instance.

permission_removal_guide._comment: Use this guide to scope down the policy for environments that do not need all features.
